assessments.name: Template Properties
assessments.description: For component state used in templates, use signals (preferred) or RxJS observables. Do not use plain class properties for template-bound state.

outputFiles.code:
{{ product()?.name \ }}

{{ product()?.description \ }}

${{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) \ }}

attemptDetails.reasoning:
We need to apply the rule to the provided file. It's an Angular component that reads a signal property named `product` which currently is a normal property assigned inside subscription. The template uses product?.name etc. Must convert to signal. The violation location is given: Location: 12-12 (presumably line 12 in app.ts). The full file content includes app.ts and app.html. We must modify both files appropriately, applying minimal changes. According to rule: update template first, then component TS. Thus we need to produce updated file content with modifications. We need to identify the property: product is assigned in ngOnInit via subscription. So we need to change to a signal using toSignal. Implementation steps: - Import Signal and toSignal from @angular/core. - Change property type to Signal perhaps. - Use toSignal(this.productService.getProduct()) maybe with initialValue: null. - Remove subscription and ngOnInit method? According to rule: remove old property assignment, if nothing else remains in callback/method body, remove entire callback/method. Also remove implements OnInit if applicable. We need to keep component class, maybe still implements OnInit? Could remove implements OnInit. Also need to adjust template: replace every occurrence of product?.name, product?.description, product?.price?.toFixed(2), product?.image, product?.name alt etc with product()?.name etc. Also change interpolation bindings. In template, there are attribute bindings: [attr.src]="product?.image" and [alt]="product?.name". Also interpolation: {{ product?.name }}, {{ product?.description }}, {{ product?.price?.toFixed(2) }}. We need to change these to product()?.image, product()?.name, product()?.description, product()?.price()?.toFixed(2)? Actually price is a number; product?.price is a number, but product()?.price will be a number? The signal returns Product | null; product may have price: number. So product()?.price is number, can call toFixed via method? toFixed is method on number, can be called directly: product()?.price?.toFixed(2). That's fine. Thus template modifications: each occurrence of product?.X becomes product()?.X. Also need to replace product?.name with product()?.name inside alt attribute. Now component TS: Replace product property declaration with Signal = toSignal(...). Since subscription uses productService.getProduct() observable; we can use toSignal(this.productService.getProduct()). Use initialValue: null. Remove ngOnInit method and subscription. Also remove product property initial value and assignment. Also need to remove import of OnInit maybe. Also need to adjust change detection? Not needed. Now final file content: Provide both app.ts and app.html updated. The user wants "Return the FULL updated file." Probably they want the full file content with modifications. Since there are multiple files, we need to output the updated file content for each file indicated. The "Files" section lists src/app/app.ts and src/app/app.html. We need to output the entire content of each file after changes. Thus we should output something like: ### src/app/app.ts Location: 12-12 (maybe not needed in output but we can include). Then Full File Content: updated code. ### src/app/app.html Full File Content: updated code. But the instruction: "Return the FULL updated file." Maybe they want the whole file content for each file. Thus we will output a block with the updated file content for each file. We must not explain changes. Thus produce something like: ``` ### src/app/app.ts Location: 12-12 Full File Content ``` But the format earlier shows they embed file name and then Full File Content. We can follow similar. Probably just output the updated code for both files. Let's craft updated app.ts: ``` import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, inject, Signal } from "@angular/core"; import { toSignal } from "@angular/core/rxjs-interop"; import { Product } from "./product.model"; import { ProductService } from "./product.service"; @Component({ selector: "app-root", templateUrl: "./app.html", styleUrl: "./app.css", changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.Default, }) export class App { private readonly productService = inject(ProductService); protected userSignal: Signal = toSignal( this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null }, ); } ``` But the property name is product; we can keep productSignal variable named product maybe. Better: product: Signal = toSignal(this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null }); Thus: ``` protected product: Signal = toSignal(this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null }); ``` But the class doesn't need to implement OnInit. Thus final app.ts: ``` import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, inject, Signal } from "@angular/core"; import { toSignal } from "@angular/core/rxjs-interop"; import { Product } from "./product.model"; import { ProductService } from "./product.service"; @Component({ selector: "app-root", templateUrl: "./app.html", styleUrl: "./app.css", changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.Default, }) export class App { private readonly productService = inject(ProductService); protected product: Signal = toSignal( this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null }, ); } ``` We removed ngOnInit and subscription. Now app.html modifications: Change each occurrence. Original template lines: ``` ``` Change to: ``` ``` Then: ```

{{ product?.name \}}

``` Change to: ```

{{ product()?.name \}}

``` Similarly: ```

{{ product?.description \}}

``` Change to: ```

{{ product()?.description \}}

``` And: ```
${{ product?.price?.toFixed(2) \}}
``` Change to: ```
${{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) \}}
``` Make sure to keep escaping backslashes? The original had backslashes before } maybe for escaping? It seems they used `{{ product?.name \}}` with a backslash before closing braces? Actually it's weird; maybe they had spaces? We'll preserve same formatting but replace property?. with product()?. Thus final app.html: ```

{{ product()?.name \}}

{{ product()?.description \}}

${{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) \}}
``` Now produce output with file sections. Thus final answer should include both file updates. We must not explain. Proceed.
